subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV/EFF, World Bank, ADB, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Sri Lankan official (Census/Stats, Central Bank of Sri Lanka) + Sri Lankan independent press (Daily Mirror, Daily FT, Groundviews).

A post-default recovery and democratic reset: a 2022 sovereign default and mass uprising, followed by a 2024 election that brought a reform/anti-corruption government to power with a parliamentary supermajority.

Standout features: a major monetary/inflation stabilisation and a democratic reset, against a high-debt and demographic-decline overhang and severe climate exposure. Source-lean: HIGH Anglophone; Sinhala/Tamil sources under-read.

economic.notes: A deep-hole recovery with a major monetary/inflation stabilisation, against a high-debt overhang.

fiscal_state.notes: Defaulted in 2022 but with the IMF program advancing and debt restructuring near-complete, a primary surplus returning, though debt remains high. (IMF)

monetary_independence.notes: The central bank gained legal independence via a 2023 act, with a credible disinflation framework - a genuine reform win. (CBSL)

inflation.notes: Collapsed from a ~70% crisis peak to near target - a major stabilisation success. (CBSL)

banking_stability.notes: Recapitalising post-crisis with recovering private credit and legacy NPLs. (ADB; IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Tea, apparel, tourism, and remittances with rebuilt reserves and a tourism rebound, against US-tariff and China-debt exposure; no resource curse. (ADB; IMF)

institutional.notes: An improving, reform-mandated trajectory from a weak base.

rule_of_law.notes: A weak corruption legacy, with a 2024-mandated anti-corruption drive criticised as incremental. (WJP; ICG)

statistical_integrity.notes: Usable and improving in transparency under the IMF program. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Weak but slowly digitalising and reforming. (East Asia Forum)

press_freedom.notes: Improved post-2022, with an Online Safety Act chill persisting. (RSF; HRW)

political_social.notes: A genuine democratic reset, against unresolved reconciliation and a demographic drag.

political_stability.notes: A 2022 mass uprising, followed by a 2024 election that brought a reform/anti-corruption government to power with a parliamentary supermajority - a genuine democratic reset, relatively stable now. (ICG; Diplomat)

civil_liberties.notes: Improving, though anti-terror-law use actually rose in 2025. (HRW; OHCHR)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Limited routes and tourism-visa-friendly; not a relocation destination. (observed)

social_cohesion.notes: No active conflict, but Tamil reconciliation and devolution remain stalled amid crisis hardship. (HRW; OHCHR)

demographics.notes: An ageing population after a fertility collapse, with heavy skilled emigration - a structural long-term drag. (Census; UNFPA)

geopolitical.notes: A non-aligned India-China balancer with no active conflict.

alliance_reliability.notes: Non-aligned, pragmatically balancing India and China. (ICG)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active conflict since the 2009 civil-war end. (HRW)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Crisis-era controls easing under the IMF program; unsanctioned. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: A historically strong health system, against severe climate exposure and thin catastrophe cover.

climate.notes: Island monsoon variability, flood/drought/landslide, coastal/sea-level exposure, with readiness strained by fiscal crisis. (ND-GAIN; IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: A historically strong universal system, crisis-strained but recovering. (ADB; UNFPA)

infrastructure.notes: Decent, with power cuts eased, against China-debt white-elephant risk and disaster fragility. (Mongabay)

crime_safety.notes: Relatively low violent crime - a strength. (comparative)

disaster_insurance.notes: Very low penetration against flood/cyclone/tsunami exposure. (World Bank)

summary: A country climbing out of a deep hole: after a 2022 sovereign default and mass uprising, an IMF program and near-complete debt restructuring have returned a primary surplus, the newly-independent central bank has crushed inflation from a ~70% peak to near target, and a 2024 election delivered a reform/anti-corruption government with a parliamentary supermajority - a genuine democratic reset. The overhang is real: still-high debt, stalled Tamil reconciliation, an ageing population with heavy skilled emigration, and severe climate exposure (a destructive 2025 cyclone) against very thin catastrophe insurance. The outcome distribution is balanced between recovery upside and structural drag.
